question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retryWhen(Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, interval).maxBackoff(interval.multipliedBy(4L))) in subscribe() to cause an indefinite retry loop by continuously injecting malformed MessagePack payloads into the staking reward Redis channel, ensuring the listener never stabilizes and staking rewards are never delivered?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the String.format('topic.%d', filter.getTopicId().getId()) channel naming to subscribe to the exact same Redis channel used by the importer's RedisPublisher (which uses TOPIC_FORMAT = 'topic.%d'), receiving raw staking reward TopicMessage objects before any filtering is applied, leaking unpaid reward data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of message integrity verification in the messageSerializer (Jackson2JsonRedisSerializer with MessagePackFactory) to inject a TopicMessage with a crafted message field that encodes staking reward manipulation instructions, causing downstream consumers to process fraudulent reward data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the prefetch parameter in publishOn(Schedulers.boundedElastic(), false, listenerProperties.getPrefetch()) to cause staking reward messages to be prefetched and buffered in memory for an attacker-controlled subscriber, allowing the attacker to observe reward settlement timing before legitimate subscribers process the messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: unsubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that unsubscribe() only calls topicMessages.remove() without cancelling the underlying Redis subscription, causing a zombie subscription that continues to consume Redis resources and potentially delivers staking reward messages to a detached Flux that no legitimate subscriber can access?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared Flux produced by subscribe() combined with the .share() operator to perform a slow-consumer attack on the staking reward topic, where the attacker's slow consumption causes backpressure that delays staking reward message delivery to all co-subscribers, creating a window for reward manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of authorization checks in getSharedListener() to subscribe to any staking reward topic ID without proving ownership of the corresponding account, receiving all staking reward settlement messages for accounts they do not own and using that information to steal unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the container.flatMapMany() call in subscribe() to trigger multiple concurrent Redis subscriptions to the same staking reward channel by racing the container Mono's cache(), causing duplicate message delivery that confuses staking reward accounting and leads to double-payment or theft?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: RedisTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Micrometer observation tap on the container Mono to infer the timing and frequency of staking reward topic subscriptions through side-channel metric leakage, enabling them to correlate reward settlement events with specific accounts and target unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on gRPC topic subscriptions to open thousands of connections to the staking reward topic channel, causing the ConcurrentHashMap topicMessages to be populated with a single shared Flux that is overwhelmed by subscriber demand, dropping staking reward messages and leaving rewards unpaid?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the integer overflow in filter.getTopicId().getId() when the topic ID is at Long.MAX_VALUE, causing String.format('topic.%d') to produce a channel name that wraps around to a valid staking reward topic channel, intercepting reward messages intended for a different topic?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnComplete() handler calling unsubscribe() to force a subscription teardown by publishing a Redis channel completion signal, causing all subscribers on the staking reward topic to lose their Flux and miss subsequent reward settlement messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that computeIfAbsent() in getSharedListener() does not validate the TopicMessageFilter's topicId against a whitelist of legitimate staking reward topics, allowing subscription to internal system topics that carry staking reward calculation data not intended for public consumption?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MessagePack deserialization of TopicMessage objects to perform a type confusion attack, where a crafted payload causes the deserializer to map staking reward fields to incorrect TopicMessage properties, resulting in corrupted reward amounts being delivered to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onBackpressureBuffer with BufferOverflowStrategy.ERROR in SharedTopicListener.listen() to deliberately trigger buffer overflow on a staking reward topic by flooding it with messages, causing the subscriber's stream to terminate with an error and the subscriber to miss the actual staking reward settlement message?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of message ordering guarantees in the Redis pub/sub channel to deliver staking reward TopicMessage objects out of consensus timestamp order, causing the filterMessage check (message.getConsensusTimestamp() >= filter.getStartTime()) to incorrectly filter out valid reward messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: RedisTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of the container Mono (Mono.defer()) to cause a race condition where multiple threads simultaneously attempt to create a ReactiveRedisMessageListenerContainer, resulting in multiple containers subscribing to the same staking reward channel and delivering duplicate reward messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the shared Flux in subscribe() uses .share() without autoConnect, meaning the upstream Redis subscription is only active while at least one subscriber is present, to create a subscription gap by briefly disconnecting all subscribers during a staking reward settlement window, causing the reward message to be permanently lost?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TopicMessageFilter.startTime validation (isValidStartTime() requires startTime <= DomainUtils.now()) to set startTime to the current time, subscribing to the staking reward topic just before a known reward settlement event and receiving the settlement message while other subscribers with earlier startTimes are filtered out?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: unsubscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the timing between topicMessages.remove() in unsubscribe() and a concurrent computeIfAbsent() call in getSharedListener() to cause a new Flux to be created that starts a fresh Redis subscription, missing staking reward messages published during the re-subscription window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retryWhen backoff strategy in subscribe() to cause a predictable reconnection pattern that creates a known gap in staking reward message delivery, timing their own reward claim transaction to occur during this gap when the mirror node's listener is not receiving settlement confirmations?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getSharedListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of topic ID validation in getSharedListener() to subscribe to topic ID 0 (which maps to 'topic.0'), potentially intercepting system-level staking reward messages that are published to the default or null topic channel?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Message::getMessage mapping in subscribe() which extracts only the message body without validating the channel name, allowing a message published to a different Redis channel to be delivered as if it came from the staking reward topic channel, injecting fraudulent reward data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/RedisTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: RedisTopicListener()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ObservationRegistry integration (Micrometer.observation) on the container Mono to cause observation context leakage that reveals internal staking reward topic subscription metadata, enabling the attacker to identify high-value reward accounts and target their unpaid rewards?
